fact: August 7 is exactly 219 days into the year, which means there are only 146 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is peak butterfly season - monarchs are getting ready for their epic 3,000-mile journey to Mexico!
The sun rises around 6 AM and sets around 8 PM in most of North America, giving you about 14 hours of daylight to play outside.
August 7 falls smack in the middle of Leo season - people born on this day are supposedly as brave and dramatic as lions!
Cicadas are screaming their loudest right now in many places, making that weird buzzing sound that can reach 120 decibels - louder than a chainsaw!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, August 7 is deep winter, with temperatures that can drop below freezing.
This date sits exactly 31 days after the summer solstice in the north, meaning daylight is already getting noticeably shorter each day.
Corn plants in the American Midwest are at their tallest right now - some stalks tower over 8 feet high, way taller than most adults!
The Perseid meteor shower is ramping up around August 7, so you might spot shooting stars if you look up at night.
In many northern regions, this is when lakes and rivers hit their warmest temperatures of the entire year - perfect for swimming!
August 7 marks the time when many trees start secretly preparing for fall by slowing down their green chlorophyll production.
Honeybees are working overtime right now, with worker bees visiting up to 5,000 flowers per day to stock up for winter!
